prompt: Why can an attacker abuse this form from another website?
code_snippet:
<form action="/transfer" method="POST"> ... </form>
<!-- no anti-CSRF token; relies on the session cookie -->

options.text: The input is not sanitised, allowing script injection
The id parameter can be incremented to reach other records
The server fetches an attacker-controlled URL
The browser auto-attaches the session cookie to cross-site requests, so a forged request from another site executes as the victim

explanation: Cookies are sent automatically on cross-site requests, so an attacker's page can submit this state-changing form and the victim's authenticated cookie makes it succeed — Cross-Site Request Forgery (CWE-352). This is about *ambient cookie authority*, not script injection (XSS) or object ids (IDOR).
